event_info: 138.4ks fuller and hint of away swing; Rahane stays rooted to the crease and opens the bat face; guides it down to third man and gets off the mark
140.4ks shortens the length and swings away; Allen has a loose waft and is beaten on the outside-edge
139.3ks fuller and shaping away; Allen's feet don't move and he looks to drive; again beaten on the outside-edge. Pant dives to his right to glove the ball. Plenty of swing for Shami..
uhhh! Shami is swinging it both ways. This is the inswinger; Allen charges down and looks to loft through the line; the ball beats the inside-edge this time
140ks back of a length and swinging away; Allen aims to punch and gets a thick outside-edge to third man
shot! 137ks full pitcher around off; right in the slot; and Rahane drives through the line. He hits it uppishly and evades the outstretched left hand of Shami

138ks delivery straying down leg; too far for Allen to connect with the glance and the ball keeps swirling as it goes behind; Pant moves across and gets into the tangle; manages to get a glove and stop
not convincing but Allen has his first boundary. 136.3ks back of a length angled in; Allen uses the angle and whips it backward of square. The ball races away..
back to back fours. Same region and again not off the middle. Fraction short around off; Allen drags it off the inner half and beats short fine to the left
136.9ks low full toss on off; Allen drives and picks out extra cover

ball_event: out Caught by Digvesh Singh Rathi!! Another failure for Allen. Prince strikes in his first over. LSG have an early wicket. That's a well-judged catch by Rathi. 137.6ks back of a length delivery
event_info: swings away a bit and Allen looks to heave it across the line; gets a thick top-edge and the ball flies down to third man. It seemed to be heading over the rope but Rathi steadies himself right on the edge of the rope; stands sideways to his left and takes it with both hands. He was very close to the ad cushions but did well not to step on it. Finn Allen c Digvesh Singh Rathi b Prince Yadav 9(8) [4s-2]
138ks back of a length and swinging away; Raghuvanshi opens the bat face and guides it to deep backward point
136.6ks on a length and close to off; Rahane eases it to cover-point
138.9ks full and swings away; Raghuvanshi drives with the movement and finds cover
141ks on a length and around off; Raghuvanshi meets it on the rise and creams it straight to cover-point
140.3ks good length ball close to off; Raghuvanshi stays back and guides it wide of slip. Marsh dives to his right and makes the stop

shot! Three dots and Raghuvanshi decides to do something different. He makes a bit of room and meets the good length delivery on the up; lofts through the line and hammers it over extra cover; one bounce and over the ropes
141ks delivery following a backing away Raghuvanshi; no room to work with and he has to settle with a knock to cover

Rathi is on fire with his fielding. He took a good catch and now saves some runs. Slower delivery; in the slot and Raghuvanshi drives it firmly towards mid-on. Rathi dives to his left and parries it towards mid-off
flighted delivery on middle; Raghuvanshi pushes it to cover
darted down leg; Raghuvanshi misses the sweep and Pant does not stop cleanly
shortens the length and drifts in; Rahane tucks it to square leg
fabulous shot! 99.8ks pushed through; keeps coming with the angle; Rahane comes down the track; gives himself room and lofts it inside out over extra cover. The timing takes it all the way..
flatter and at the stumps; Rahane turns it to the right of Siddharth and crosses over
98.4ks short of length around off; Raghuvanshi stays leg-side and punches it to cover-point
sliding down leg; Raghuvanshi drags the sweep straight to short fine
98.6ks short of length outside off; Rahane gets on top of the bounce and mistimes the punch to cover
100.4ks fired fuller and around off; Rahane works it wristily and picks out square leg
uhhh! Flatter and fraction short; Rahane crouches low and aims to pull; ekes out an inside-edge and the ball gets lodged in the back pad
Rahane gets this away. Rathi missed his length - overpitched around off; Rahane makes a bit of room and lofts straight down the ground. Not off the middle but enough to beat the long-on fielder
back to back fours. Once again Rathi is full in his length; Rahane backs away and opens the bat face; slices the lofted drive over backward point and the ball trickles into the rope
fuller and drifting in; Rahane works it wide of long-on and scampers back for the second
uhhh! Still something on offer for the pacers. Back of a length and in the channel; Raghuvanshi looks to punch off the back foot but the ball nips in to beat the inside-edge
1 run; shuffles across and sits down to play the lap/sweep; gets a low full toss and misses the shot; the ball deflects off the pad into the leg-side
134.3ks full toss outside off; Rahane creams the drive but Mukul denies him a boundary - dives full stretch to his right and parries it to mid-off. He saved three runs..
short of length outside off; ample width on offer for Raghuvanshi to play the upper cut; he goes over the backward point region and picks up a much needed boundary

Kolkata Knight Riders on the move. Avesh overcompensates with his length - feeds it in the slot and Raghuvanshi desposts it into the stands. Full pitcher around off; Raghuvanshi stays leg-side and gets under the length; lofts it handsomely and sends it sailing over deep extra cover

15 from the last powerplay over. Not much wrong with the delivery but a terrific shot. Good length ball close to off; Raghuvanshi gets on top of the bounce and crunches the drive in front of square; finds the small gap between cover-point and cover and picks up the boundary
97.3ks drifting in from round the wicket; Rahane nudges it through mid-wicket
99.9ks quicker and fuller; Raghuvanshi drives in front of square and Rathi had some ground to cover from sweeper; easy couple
Raghuvanshi on the move now. This is full and sliding in; Raghuvanshi sits down and gets under the length; sweeps it behind square and beats the fielder in the deep
flatter and skidding in; Raghuvanshi carves it to backward point and Marsh makes a diving stop
102.3ks fired at the stumps; Rahane nurdles it backward of square leg
100.4ks fullish delivery on middle; Raghuvanshi makes room and drives straight to cover
95.9ks googly around off; Rahane check drives it down to long-off

1 run; appeal for LBW. Pant and Rathi have a chat. The skipper gestures the impact is outside off. Raghuvanshi attempts the reverse sweep and gets himself outside off-stump. He misses the shot and the ball rolls off the pad towards backward point
what timing! Just a clip an the ball races away. Bit more flight on this delivery; drifts into leg-stump; Rahane picks it off his pads and splits the two fielders perfectly in the deep
95.6ks tossed up on off; Rahane works it into the mid-wicket pocket and Rathi chases it himself
99.2ks shortens the length around off; Raghuvanshi punches to sweeper cover
94.7ks slows it up a bit; draws Rahane forward and the flick is chipped to the right of Rathi. He was moving away in his followthrough and had no chance of catching that

whoa! That's an incredible shot. Rahane loses control of his bat and it flies towards square leg; but the ball lands beyond the deep backward point fence. Plenty of width on offer and Rahane literally threw his bat at the slash; sliced it behind square and sent it all the way
131.5ks full and outside off; Rahane creams the drive straight to sweeper cover
116.9ks slower delivery on off; Raghuvanshi tucks it into the vacant mid-wicket pocket and the pair race back for the second

Avesh continues to leak runs. He has already gone for 30 in 1.4 overs. 119.9ks off-pace delivery on the pads and Raghuvanshi has no problems putting that away; uses his bottom hand and whips it over backward square leg
131.7ks full pitcher around off; Raghuvanshi drives and picks out extra cover
111.4ks slower ball around off; Raghuvanshi carves it straight to backward point
135.2ks fraction short at the stumps; Rahane drags the pull to fine leg
wayward slower ball bouncer; Raghuvanshi moves inside the line and lets it pass. Called wide for height
137.9ks very full and close to yorker length; Raghuvanshi digs it back to Prince
leg-lined delivery; Raghuvanshi flicks it backward of square
102.6ks slower leg-cutter; Rahane knocks it straight down the ground
poor delivery and Raghuvanshi puts it away. Full toss down leg; he just had to place it and he does that with ease; glances it fine and beats Mukul's dive near the rope
135.5ks short of length delivery; Raghuvanshi is cramped for room and mistimes the pull to deep square leg
93.7ks flighted on middle; Raghuvanshi eases it wide of cover

ball_event: out Caught by Mohammed Shami!! Rathi breaks the stand. Rahane is asked to wait. But Shami nodded looking at Rahane or Pant after taking the catch. He was confident that it carried. The third umpire is having a look. The finger seems to be under the ball. "Satisfied the ball is gone straight into the hands
event_info: says KN Anantha Padmanabhan. OUT flashes on the giantscreen. This was not a wicket-taking delivery but Rathi and LSG will take it. Half tracker outside off; Rahane decides to go leg-side; drags the pull to mid-wicket and Shami bends down; scoops it inches above the ground. Ajinkya Rahane c Mohammed Shami b Digvesh Singh Rathi 41(24) [4s-4 6s-2]
googly from short of length; Green picked it off the pitch and ekes out an inside-edge adjacent to the wicket on the leg-side
90.9ks floated up outside off; Green drives to long-off
back of a length on off; Raghuvanshi stays leg-side and directs it square on the off-side
96.2ks drifting into leg; Green eases it to long-on. First over in the innings that has not gone for a boundary; informs Harsha Bhogle on air
tossed up around off; Green opens the bat face and pushes to sweeper cover
101.3ks quicker and drifting in; Raghuvanshi sits down and sweeps backward of square leg
sliding in with the angle; Green clips it behind square on the leg-side

ball_event: out Caught by Aiden Markram!! This is a massive moment. Both set batters sent back in consecutive overs. Raghuvanshi knows what he has done and throws his bat up in anguish. 101.9ks fuller and drifting in
event_info: Raghuvanshi comes down the track and gets too close to the ball; hence he does not get the elevation to clear long-on and picks out Markram perfectly. A good knock but Raghuvanshi would have wanted his third fifty of the season. Angkrish Raghuvanshi c Aiden Markram b Manimaran Siddharth 45(33) [4s-5 6s-2]
flatter delivery on leg; Rinku flicks it to deep mid-wicket
96.5ks touch slower and drifting in; Green knocks it to the right of Siddharth
first ball against Shami and Rinku decides to give the charge; makes room as well but ends up chopping it behind stumps; to the right of Pant and the duo cross over
driven down to long-off
135.7ks angled across the left-hander; Rinku steers it to third man
124.8ks slower ball close to off; Green stabs it down the pitch and past Shami
125ks another off-pace delivery; Rinku stays leg-side and eases it to cover
136.9ks low full toss outside off; Rinku drives square of the wicket and retains strike
starts with a full ball from round the wicket; Rinku drives towards cover off the bottom of the bat

ball_event: out Bowled!! Oh lazy from Rinku. He stood back in the crease to try and steer this fine to third man. Was a length delivery angling in
event_info: Rinku thought he had the line covered but he was well inside and missed it completely. Balls hit the top of off stump. He will be livid with himself for getting out this way. Rinku Singh b Avesh Khan 4(7)
oh Powell wanted to pull-guide this behind square; ended up playing way too early and got an under edge that rolled back to the bowler
beaten on the pull now! Powell is being troubled by the short length. This was slightly down the leg-side but still well inside for a wide

another under edge! Smart of Avesh Khan to use this dry pitch and bowl into the wicket. Rovman Powell has not come to terms with the pitch yet and has not middled a ball since arrival. Tried to cut this short of length delivery outside off stump; under edge rolled to the keeper
now he wants to drive on the rise but the ball isn't coming on and the ball rolls to long-on off the inside edge
short delivery on the leg stump; Powell pulls through deep square leg
turning away; Green looks to flick; ball takes the leading edge and rolls back to the bowler
plays a cute back foot defence. That's interesting
and again! He wants to play him out. Just another defence
manages to tuck it along the ground through long-on
not sure which way it's going to turn so comes forward; reads the spin off the pitch and manages to get an edge that rolls towards short third man
finally they show some intent. Advances and manages to loft through the line and sends it soaring over long-on. Crowd gets their voices back
very full; the yorker; Powell could only dig it out
wanted to flick it away. Oh off the under edge and no chance for RP. Unlucky for the bowler but KKR won't mind how they come. Ball escapes fine to the fine leg fence
driven hard down the ground but smashes the stumps at the bowler's end but Green hasn't moved out of his crease
full delivery; Powell flicks to deep mid-wicket
advances but Manimaran Siddharth cramps him for room with a flatter delivery; Green pushes it to long-off
killer yorker; Green gets his boot out of the way to avoid damage and manages to squeeze it out to point
almost gets another yorker right; Powell taps it to long-off

goes for the change up and tries the slower ball bumper. Problem was he had both backward point and third man up so Green smartly guides the ball between the two and gets the much needed four. Prince Yadav shakes his head in disappointment for not getting his tactic right
bowls an angry yorker now and gets it right; dug out for one
belted down the ground! Just missed the yorker and it's in Powell's arc. Front leg out of the way for a muscular smack down the ground past the ump for four more

started as a great over but three hit-me balls and KKR are up and running again. The yorker is a difficult ball to execute. Even legends like Malinga; Bumrah don't get every single yorker right. Prince Yadav tries another one and dishes out a low full toss; Powell says thank you very much and smashes it over mid-wicket
full and outside off stump; Green looks to drive; inside edge rolls to short fine leg
well bowled short ball; Powell was troubled on the pull. Mistimes it uppishly and it lands safely at backward square leg. Rushes back for two

yorker-turned-full toss. Powell is just waiting for these gifts to accelerate. He wasn't middling a single ball bowled into the wicket. Pressure on the bowler to get his strategy right. Is it the yorker or is it cutters into the wicket? Avesh changed the length and tried the yorker; only to leak four runs
now he tries the wide yorker; too wide says the ump
low full toss and he got away with it. Powell lost shape on the shot and mistimed to long-on

smart short ball to surprise Green. He was expecting a yorker but Avesh Khan bowled into the wicket; got it to fly through interestingly. Green swung his bat at it and missed. Green's head height is 2.03m; ball actual height is 2.01m so it's not a wide on height. But they want to check the width as well. That's inside the line as well
slot ball in the name of trying the full ball. Green flicks and he sends it wide of long-on to the right and it races away for a much needed boundary
1 run; very full delivery; Powell misses the flick; ball hits the pad and rolls to point
tries the slower ball but offers slot. Yeah that will help by strike-rate says Green; who gets his front leg away to muscle it high over long-on. Lets out a smile after that connection

the ump hasn't given it this time and KKR review again. This seems higher. Head height is 2.03m; actual ball height is 2.12m so it's a clear wide. Shami tried to bowl the fast bouncer but it was too high
very full delivery; Green drills it to long-off
great yorker aiming base of middle and leg. Powell looks to flick; inside edge hits the pad and rolls to the off-side as Powell tries to take his foot out of the way
great yorker outside off stump; Green digs it out to backward point
he's nailed a leg stump yorker; Powell could only scoop it out to deep mid-wicket
clever! LSG felt they didn't need a fine leg. Powell spotted the field. Knew Prince Yadav is going to aim for the yorker; got down low and paddle-swept it for four

that's well over the head and a definite wide. LSG chance their review just because they have two. Waste of time. Taking a while to get the graphics for the height; 1.83m is the head height and the actual ball went at 1.90m

great yorker and they have an interesting field with Mitchell Marsh being stationed between the ump and the non-striker at mid-on. The kind of field Dhoni has to Pollard and the field they had in the WI-SA game in the 2021 T20 World Cup
oh unlucky! Yorker; almost beat Green but he swung hard to get an inside edge and the ball escaped to the fine leg fence
yorker; Green slogs it to deep mid-wicket
swung too hard and missed. Lovely slower ball; used the pitch; Powell swung early and missed

1 run; another great slower ball into the pitch and it's denied KKR some 10 odd runs in this last over. Could be crucial in the run chance or defence. Powell couldn't lay bat on this slog and they steal a bye

Mitch Marsh attacks straightaway. What else you expect from him. Vaibhav dug this in and bowled a bit too short. The problem was the extra width available for Marsh to rock back and crack a nice cut shot off the back foot. Raced past point
it's more good length; it's tighter; so Marsh respects with a defence
pushed to mid-off
good length delivery on the stumps; Markram defends off the back foot
oh lovely shape back in. Jumped on the batter too as Markram bent his back knee to guide the ball late; ball hits the splice of the bat instead and rolls towards cover
full delivery on the stumps; Markram flicks to mid-wicket
very full delivery; drives towards long-off
fired down the leg-side
flatter delivery on the stumps; Marsh defends off the back foot
full and angling in with zero room; Marsh calmly taps it to long-off
nicely flicked away off the back foot but it's cut off by the man at deep mid-wicket
1 run; got away with the leg-side full delivery; Marsh tried to slog-sweep; ball rolls to short fine leg off the pad
another one past the leg-side
full delivery; drilled to long-off
flicked away over mid-wicket. The ball had some extra bounce but he still managed to time it well enough to clear the infield and once it's into those practice pitches it escapes usually to the fence

now he times it better and sends it soaring over deep mid-wicket. Short of a length delivery on the pads; Markram flicks and times it sweetly off the middle of the bat. Not the start from Saini KKR wanted after two good overs
that's drilled past mid-off and LSG are rolling away nicely. Full delivery outside off stump; Markram leans into the shot and again; the timing it perfect
1 run; slower delivery slightly missed leg; Markram tries the flick and gets beaten; ball hits the pad
taps it to point and they get a run
slower delivery on the leg stump; Markram flicks to deep mid-wicket
flatter delivery; Marsh pushes it to long-off
oh almost rushed on the flick. Skidded onto the batter; Markram just managed to flick in time to deep mid-wicket
great yorker; Marsh manages to toe-end it to long-off
this is a great over so far. Yorker just around the off stump; Markram squeezes it behind square but they have square leg close to backward square leg and it's cut off
could only drill it to long-off

this game is cruel on the bowler. The only ball Anukul Roy missed the length and what could have been a cheap over becomes a 10-run over. Offered this fullish and Marsh got down low for a slog-sweep; good hit over square leg

ball_event: out Caught by Rovman Powell!! It's the ball that's into the wicket that's got them the wicket. Look at Vaibhav Arora roar with a double fist pump. Short delivery
event_info: Markram thought he could deposit this deep beyond mid-wicket. But KKR had a man at the fence and the moment it went off the outer half of the bat; it didn't have the distance and holed out. Smart strategy from KKR to prise this wicket. Apart from that Saini over; they have actually been quite good. Aiden Markram c Rovman Powell b Vaibhav Arora 22(15) [4s-2 6s-1]
short of length delivery on the off stump; flicked to square leg

ball_event: out Caught by Angkrish Raghuvanshi!! This is a strangle down the leg-side. Not the best of balls as it was short and down the leg-side. Marsh tried to pull and gets the glove on it. Angkrish Raghuvanshi makes no mistake and Adrian Holdstock rightly raised the finger. Marsh wears a wry smile as he walks off. Very unfortunate for him. KKR have made a mega comeback and Vaibhav definitely seems to have a good understanding of what lengths to bowl. He is determied to bowl into the wicket and use this dry pitch. Mitchell Marsh c Angkrish Raghuvanshi b Vaibhav Arora 15(11) [4s-1 6s-1]
event_info: out Caught by Angkrish Raghuvanshi!! This is a strangle down the leg-side. Not the best of balls as it was short and down the leg-side. Marsh tried to pull and gets the glove on it. Angkrish Raghuvanshi makes no mistake and Adrian Holdstock rightly raised the finger. Marsh wears a wry smile as he walks off. Very unfortunate for him. KKR have made a mega comeback and Vaibhav definitely seems to have a good understanding of what lengths to bowl. He is determied to bowl into the wicket and use this dry pitch. Mitchell Marsh c Angkrish Raghuvanshi b Vaibhav Arora 15(11) [4s-1 6s-1]
short delivery down leg; Badoni looks to pull and misses. Btw; Badoni in as impact sub for Siddharth

top edgeeee; man at fine leg and it just clears him. Fifth ball into the pitch this over. Badoni gets inside the line and tries the pull shot; top edge sails over the rope. Could have so easily been the third wicket of the over as well
short delivery down the leg-side; Badoni guides the pull to fine leg
flatter delivery; Badoni defends off the back foot
flatter delivery; Badoni taps it off the back foot to long-on
hints at shuffling in the crease and then tucks this flatter delivery to square leg. He sprints to see if there is two but three KKR fielders converge to put the fear in the batters
lovely back foot guide from Badoni. Let this flatter delivery come; creamed if through cover-point and he gets the boundary needed of the over
hits the pad but missing leg so no appeal from KKR
on the leg stump; Badoni flicks to square leg
it's racing off the bat every time he's guiding off the back foot. Kartik Tyagi offered width on this short of length delivery and it rocketed past point to take third man out of the equation
knocked to point
good length delivery on the leg stump; Pant easily flicks to deep square leg
short of length delivery outside off stump; Badoni cuts to deep point
nicely done! Full delivery on the stumps; Pant steps out and drives to the right of mid-off for his first boundary of the day
bowls slow and into the wicket; Pant gets inside the line and helps it away over backward square leg
short and wide; turning away; Rishabh Pant goes on the back foot and cuts hard and straight to sweeper cover
tossed up full outside off; Ayush Badoni gets forward to drive and mistimes it back to Sunil Narine; off the thick inside half of the bat
the regulation off-break on middle; length delivery; Ayush Badoni goes on the back foot and tucks through the vacant mid-wicket area
good length and outside the leg-stump; Rishabh Pant closes the face of the bat and flicks to deep backward square-leg
good length on middle and leg; Ayush Badoni goes on the back foot and turns it to the backward square-leg area

good length on middle; slides on straight; Rishabh Pant closes the face of the bat too early; the thick leading-edge rolls to short third man. Nearly struck the pad first and had it done so; Pant was dead in front
short of length outside off; Ayush Badoni goes on the back foot and slashes it to sweeper cover

ball_event: out Caught by Kartik Tyagi!! Cameron Green gets the big wicket. It's the extra bounce that the Western Australian gets. Too high and it gets big on Rishabh Pant who is in no position to pull
event_info: loses his shape and slightly the bottom-hand as well; the top-edge loops to the right of short fine. Easy catch and KKR are elated. Rishabh Pant c Kartik Tyagi b Cameron Green 10(9) [4s-1]
another high bouncer; the slower-ball bouncer but it loops over the head of Nicholas Pooran who lets it go; called a wide
slower-ball and on a good length on middle; Nicholas Pooran gets forward and drops it with soft hands to the right of cover
slower-ball off-cutter on a good length outside off; Ayush Badoni goes on the back foot and whips it wide of mid-on for one

Nicholas Pooran has smashed this. It's a slower-ball on a short of length on off; Pooran gets forward and pulls it flat over deep mid-wicket; right off the middle of the bat and it flies over the fence
too many short balls this over; it's the slower-ball and dug in across Nicholas Pooran outside off; left alone and called a no-ball for height
caught off a free-hit. Won't matter. It's short and well-directed at Nicholas Pooran; who gets forward and pulls; doesn't time it and holes out to deep mid-wicket. Caught by Powell
almost gone! This is a fine delivery. The one-finger seam-up delivery and it curves back in; cuts Nicholas Pooran in half; only the extra bounce takes it over middle-stump
good length on leg-stump and middle; Nicholas Pooran gets forward to defend to backward point; opened the face at the last moment
slides it across Nicholas Pooran; full and Pooran doesn't pick which way the ball is turning; pushes forward and the ball sneaks past the outside-edge
full on leg-stump; Nicholas Pooran gets forward to flick to deep mid-wicket
full outside off; hint of away-turn; Ayush Badoni gets forward to push; slides off the open-face to backward point
tossed up full on middle; Ayush Badoni gets forward to defend to the left of Sunil Narine. That was a top over
slower-ball on a fuller length on off; Nicholas Pooran gets forward to drive to cover; picked out the fielder
slower-ball and across Nicholas Pooran outside off; on a good length; keeps low; Pooran swipes and misses
short and down leg; Nicholas Pooran lets it go and a wide called; the slower-ball that was

full and wide outside off; Nicholas Pooran gets forward and reaches for it to drive; good fielding by Rahane at long-off as he runs across and cuts it off with a dive to his right. Appreciated by the KKR dugout
slower-ball on a short of length on off; Nicholas Pooran gets forward to tap it to backward point. Says yes - but Ayush Badoni is not interested
slower-ball on a short of length just outside off; Nicholas Pooran pulls and misses; was early into the shot
slower-ball on a good length outside off; Nicholas Pooran reaches for it and cuts it wide of sweeper cover. Ramandeep the man
short of length outside off; shapes away; Ayush Badoni cuts and misses; swung a bit after passing the bat

good shot. It's short outside off; Ayush Badoni goes on the back foot and upper-cuts it over short third man; one bounce over the ropes. Knew where the fielders were. Was the slower-ball off-cutter and dug in short
short of length on middle; Ayush Badoni goes on the back foot to tuck to deep mid-wicket. Rinku was in quickly

ball_event: out Caught by Ramandeep Singh!! The short balls are working. It's dug in short at Nicholas Pooran
event_info: like Pant; he is in no position; gets big on him; still he gets forward to pull; sort of a top-edge and caught by long-on who was in from the fence. Another failure for Pooran. KKR move ahead. Nicholas Pooran c Ramandeep Singh b Kartik Tyagi 13(15) [6s-1]
beaten on the rise. Length delivery outside off; shapes away; Abdul Samad gets forward to drive and misses; flies over the outside-edge
length delivery outside off; Abdul Samad; leans forward and times the drive well; but straight to the fielder at sweeper cover
tossed up full on off; Abdul Samad gets forward to drive to long-on
wide length delivery outside off; just inside the tramline; left alone and it's a fair delivery
good sweep. Full and outside the leg-stump; Ayush Badoni kneels low and sweeps to the deep backward square-leg fence; the fielder was way too square. Kept it down too
this is wide. Anukul Roy fails the wide outside off line and Ayush Badoni takes the wide
high full-toss on off; Ayush Badoni nearly holes out; one bounce to Green at long-on
another wide outside off. Anukul Roy goes wide of the crease and fires it on a good length and well outside off; no shot

ball_event: out Bowled!! Wild slog across the line and cleaned up. Full and angled in on middle and leg
event_info: Abdul Samad goes across the line; misses; flicks the back pad and falls on the stumps. The task for LSG gets tougher. Abdul Samad b Anukul Roy 2(4)
full outside off; Mukul Choudhary gets forward to drive straight to the fielder at extra-cover; lots of bottom-hand in that
slower-ball and full outside off; mid-off was in; Ayush Badoni gets forward to loft and it's one bounce over the ropes. Clean strike
another slower-ball and again full outside off; Ayush Badoni gets forward to drive and this time he gets it wide of sweeper cover. He didn't have much distance to cover to his right; but the ball won
this slower-ball is nicely bowled. On a good length outside off; Ayush Badoni cuts and misses; the ball hardly bounced. May have been a bit of an under-edge too; doesn't carry to Angkrish Raghuvanshi
fumble from Vaibhav at backward point to his left and they come back for two. A short of length delivery outside off; off-pace; Ayush Badoni cuts and Vaibhav should've stopped that
too much width. A slower-ball on a short of length outside off; Ayush Badoni slashes and it's one bounce over the ropes to the deep backward point fence; in the gap. Third boundary of the over
one bounce to Rinku at deep mid-wicket. A slower-ball on a short of length just outside off; Ayush Badoni goes on the back foot and pulls; a flat hit
flat back of a length and sliding into middle and leg; Ayush Badoni is saved by the thick inside-edge as he goes on the back foot to tuck
full and wide outside off; Ayush Badoni reaches for it as he makes room and drives straight to the fielder at extra-cover; who is on the edge of the ring
50 for Ayush Badoni. A gift from Anukul Roy; high full-toss on off; Badoni gets forward and heaves; has the elevation to carry it all over the fence at wide long-on

ball_event: out Caught by Rinku Singh!! Six and out. Look at the roar from Angkrish Raghuvanshi. Rahane with a calm left-handed fist-pump. Timeout as well. It's full and wide outside off
event_info: makes Ayush Badoni reach out for the loft; doesn't have the elevation and once the ball is near Rinku; then you are in danger. Easy catch at long-off. With that wicket; KKR are now heavy favorites. Ayush Badoni c Rinku Singh b Anukul Roy 54(34) [4s-7 6s-2]
good length on off and middle; hint of turn away; Mohammed Shami goes on the back foot and turns it to the vacant backward short-leg area
flat good length on off; Mukul Choudhary gets forward to push it down to long-on
tossed up full on off; Mukul Choudhary gets forward to flick; the thick leading-edge rolls to short third man. Slid straight on
this turns big. Moves away with the seam; on a length; Mukul Choudhary pushes and he has no clue as the ball passes the outside-edge
a slip in. Again - the ball turns away; on a good length and shapes away; Mukul Choudhary goes on the back foot to push; so close to the outside-edge
the regulation off-break; full on off; Mukul Choudhary kneels low and sweeps to deep backward square-leg

beautiful bowling. No clue as the ball holds its line; angled in; probably the one-finger seam-up delivery; was outside off; Mohammed Shami pushes forward and the ball passes the outside-edge. Superb stuff

ball_event: out Caught by Anukul Roy!! Deserved wicket for Sunil Narine. A rare show of emotion as he punches the air with his right hand. It's the tossed up off-break on a fuller length on off
event_info: Mohammed Shami once he saw the flight; went for it; heaves and picks out deep square-leg. Right on the edge of the ropes and Anukul is a fine fielder. He takes it easily. Narine has bowled outstandingly tonight. Mohammed Shami c Anukul Roy b Sunil Narine 1(3)
short outside off; slower; Mukul Choudhary gets forward and pulls hard and straight to deep square-leg; doesn't take the run
full and speared in at the pads; following Mukul Choudhary slightly outside the leg-stump; he flicks and missesv

Ramandeep charging to his left at deep square-leg gets a hand to it but it rolls away to the cushions; under the body as Ramandeep tried his best. A slower-ball and dug in short outside off; Mukul Choudhary gets forward and pulls hard and into the gap

wow! The Helicopter is out. And it has flown over long-on. Such a wristy and swag shot that is. It's a good ball too; full and following Mukul Choudhary outside the leg-stump; he brings out the helicopter and it has gone over the fence. Need such strong forearms

Vaibhav Arora round the wicket. A high full-toss and angling away from the right-hander; just outside off; Mukul Choudhary heaves and it's one bounce to deep mid-wicket. Powell didn't charge for the catch
back over the wicket. It's a slower-ball and dug in short outside off; Avesh Khan pulls and misses

what power. This guy showing his talent. A slower-ball and dug in short outside off; well outside off; Mukul Choudhary gets across and forehands that way over long-off. That is another shot which shows his wrists. Such power in them
low full-toss outside off; Mukul Choudhary gets forward to drive and finds long-on. A fumble from Powell but they don't take the run
short of length outside off; Mukul Choudhary goes on the back foot and pulls to deep mid-wicket. Again they don't run
a juicy low full-toss outside off; Mukul Choudhary hangs back and brutalises the loft; way back over long-on. 10 rows back. Kartik Tyagi grimaces. He knows that was not the ball
short of length outside off; Mukul Choudhary goes on the back foot and pulls to deep mid-wicket. This time they take one
full outside off; Avesh Khan is not in line; he gets forward to loft and misses. That was in the slot. Avesh gestures where the ball should've gone - pointing in the direction of long-off

Mukul Choudhary gets away. A total miscue. But it falls in between Rahane and Rinku running in from long-off and long-on. It's a slower-ball and dug in short outside off; Choudhary gets forward to pull; goes straight up
another slower-ball and dug in short at Mukul Choudhary; who swings too early; off the body and rolls to short third man. Two dots in a row
LSG in this still. The cutter and dug in short; doesn't rise that high and Mukul Choudhary has pulled this flat over deep backward square-leg. Sails over the fence

slower-ball on a good length and in the slot outside off; Mukul Choudhary gets forward to loft and misses; rolls to Angkrish Raghuvanshi. That was there in the slot too and Mukul Choudhary knows it too
slower-ball and dug in short outside off; Mukul Choudhary pulls and the top-edge flies over Angkrish Raghuvanshi and the short third man; runs away for four

this is mauled over deep mid-wicket. Cameron Green too predictable there. Another slower-ball and short outside off; Mukul Choudhary nails the pull as he gets forward and it flies 10 rows back at deep mid-wicket. Some glove-love between Mukul and Avesh Khan
that's what LSG wanted. It's a short of length delivery on off; Avesh Khan goes on the back foot and tucks through the vacant mid-wicket area. Gives the strike back to Mukul Choudhary

Mukul Choudhary you beauty! This is a short of length delivery on off; he gets outside off slightly and belts the pull flat over deep backward square-leg. That was a poor delivery. Short boundary and it flies
beautiful yorker length delivery outside off; angling away from the right-hander; Mukul Choudhary drives and misses. Under the bat. Bowled to his left

again superb bowling. This is fine bowling. Fires the yorker length and it's well outside off; Mukul Choudhary shuffles across and looks to scoop; misses; is nutmegged and it's a dot. Two dots and the equation shifts again

Mukul Choudhary you beauty! What a knock. 50 for him. The scores are level. Vaibhav Arora on his haunches. KKR fans can't believe what they are seeing. It's full and wide outside off; Mukul Choudhary gets across and lofts with such power; it flies over sweeper cover. Once it left the bat; it was six. Such was the hit. Talk about timing them. Mukul points at the heavens. Vaibhav with a disconsolate expression - wondering what more could've he done with that ball. Meanwhile Rahane claps and tries to encourage Vaibhav

1 run; LSG with the heist of heists. Look at the celebrations from them. Rathi runs out and hugs Mukul Choudhary. Avesh Khan is running away in delight - arms raised in triumph. Mukul has become the unlikely hero. Angkrish Raghuvanshi had the chance to become a Jos Buttler but he fails to hit direct and the game is done. It's a good delivery; short of length and across Mukul Choudhary outside off; who pulls and Raghuvanshi had the chance to hit direct; had he done so; Avesh could've been short. Yes as I watch it again. Avesh was just short with a direct-hit as he doesn't dive. The keeper tried the over-arm throw and got it wide of the stumps. The ball hits Mukul but by then the game was over. Replays show that Raghuvanshi had the right glove off and showed his disappointment when he missed. Meanwhile Mukul with folded hands looks heavenwards and prays. Avesh with a fist-pump. Russell and Pant both look heavenwards but with contrasting emotions. What a game. What a win for LSG. Two nights in a row - the IPL has produced thrillers. What an advertisement for the league
